mission.title: Diagnose unknown execution failures when the console reports UNKNOWN_EXECUTION_FAILURE
mission.description: Repair mission for generic Kubernetes execution failures. Use it when the console preflight reports UNKNOWN_EXECUTION_FAILURE, when an operation fails without a clear root cause, or when you need a repeatable recovery workflow before retrying the original action.

steps.title: Check kubectl connectivity and capture the failing context
steps.description: Verify the active context, confirm the API server is reachable, and record the original command so later diagnostics stay tied to the same failure.
commands:
echo "LAST_COMMAND=${LAST_COMMAND:-not-set}"
kubectl config current-context
kubectl cluster-info

steps.title: Inspect recent events for the fastest clue
steps.description: Review the newest warning events in the affected namespace or across the cluster to surface scheduling, image pull, admission, and node-health problems.
commands:
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}"
if test -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"; then
  kubectl get events -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | tail -20
else
  kubectl get events -A --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | tail -20
fi

steps.title: Check node health and workload status
steps.description: Confirm cluster nodes are Ready and identify any non-running pods that align with the failed action.
commands:
kubectl get nodes -o wide
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}"
if test -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"; then
  kubectl get pods -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"
  kubectl get pods -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" --field-selector=status.phase!=Running
else
  kubectl get pods -A --field-selector=status.phase!=Running
fi

steps.title: Review describe output and recent pod logs
steps.description: If you already know the affected workload, inspect describe output and recent container logs to turn the generic failure into a concrete application or platform error.
commands:
if test -n "${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}" && test -n "${TARGET_POD:-}"; then
  kubectl describe pod -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" "$TARGET_POD"
  kubectl logs -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" "$TARGET_POD" --tail=100 --all-containers
else
  echo "Set TARGET_NAMESPACE and TARGET_POD to inspect a specific workload"
fi

steps.title: Apply common recovery actions and retry once
steps.description: After identifying the likely cause, run a targeted recovery action such as restarting a deployment, deleting a stuck pod, or draining an unhealthy node, then retry the original action one time.
commands:
if test -n "${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}" && test -n "${TARGET_DEPLOYMENT:-}"; then kubectl rollout restart deployment/"$TARGET_DEPLOYMENT" -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"; else echo "Set TARGET_NAMESPACE and TARGET_DEPLOYMENT to restart a deployment"; fi
if test -n "${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}" && test -n "${TARGET_POD:-}"; then kubectl delete pod -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" "$TARGET_POD"; else echo "Set TARGET_NAMESPACE and TARGET_POD to recreate a stuck pod"; fi
if test -n "${UNHEALTHY_NODE:-}"; then kubectl cordon "$UNHEALTHY_NODE"; else echo "Set UNHEALTHY_NODE to isolate a failing node before deeper repair"; fi

troubleshooting.title: kubectl fails before workload diagnostics start
troubleshooting.description: If kubectl cluster-info or kubectl auth whoami fails, this is probably a credential or API reachability issue. Switch to the EXPIRED_CREDENTIALS or MISSING_CREDENTIALS mission instead of continuing with workload recovery.

troubleshooting.title: Events show Forbidden or RBAC errors
troubleshooting.description: Stop the generic recovery workflow and follow the RBAC_DENIED mission. Restarting workloads will not fix an authorization problem.

troubleshooting.title: Deleting the pod does not help
troubleshooting.description: Inspect the owning Deployment, StatefulSet, or Job with kubectl describe and review its image, probes, and mounted secrets or configmaps. A recreated pod will keep failing until the controller-level issue is fixed.

resolution.summary: Cluster connectivity, events, node health, pod status, and logs were checked so UNKNOWN_EXECUTION_FAILURE can be narrowed to a concrete cause and followed by a targeted recovery action.

prerequisites.resources: Cluster read access; restart or delete actions require write access to the affected workloads.
prerequisites.description: Set TARGET_NAMESPACE, TARGET_POD, TARGET_DEPLOYMENT, LAST_COMMAND, and UNHEALTHY_NODE when you already know the failing workload or node.
